title: Market Brief — Saturday, April 18, 2026
summary: Compute is in severe shortage across legacy silicon: H100 at 11% availability (111/325), H200 at 11% (58/326), while B200 offers relative relief at 29% (171/447) but is tightening fast with utilization surging +20.9pp in 30 days. The headline move is H100 spot spiking +15.3% day-over-day to $2.00/hr, signaling demand migration as H200 utilization hits 89% and effectively walls out new workloads.
paragraphs: Compute is in severe shortage across legacy silicon: H100 at 11% availability (111/325), H200 at 11% (58/326), while B200 offers relative relief at 29% (171/447) but is tightening fast with utilization surging +20.9pp in 30 days. The headline move is H100 spot spiking +15.3% day-over-day to $2.00/hr, signaling demand migration as H200 utilization hits 89% and effectively walls out new workloads.

On TCO-adjusted B200 cost of $3.58/M tokens, medium-tier inference barely breaks even at 5% margin, but H100 and H200 medium-tier operators are underwater, losing roughly $5.70-6.20/M tokens at those price points. DeepSeek grades Burning Cash, pricing V3.2 output at $0.42/M against production floors multiples higher. OpenAI grades Neutral, extracting premium margins on o3 Pro. Anthropic grades Strong on Opus tiering. Google grades Weak, with Gemini 2.5 Pro priced generically at $10/M output with no differentiation.

B200 spot at $3.91/hr looks undervalued given 96.1% annualized vol and rapid utilization ramp, offering the best risk-adjusted inference margin in the stack. H100 at $2.00/hr post-spike is overvalued for new capacity buildout; sell H100 exposure and rotate into B200 futures before availability drops below 20%.
bullets: 11% of H100 GPUs are available, classifying the market as a severe shortage.
58 of 326 H200 GPUs are free, a severe 11% availability rate with utilization up 12.7pp in 30 days.
$0.42/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss of approximately $3.16/M against TCO-adjusted H100 cost.
96.1% annualized volatility on B200 OCPI makes it the most volatile GPU class by a factor of two.
$2.50/hr TCO-adjusted H100 cost means medium-tier token sellers lose $5.85/M on every Llama 70B request.
21.43 on the LLMflation Index means API pricing has fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
